task_completion.description: Verify 'Obergefell v. Hodges' is moved to the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot where the Jurism 'Trash' folder is selected in the left pane, 'Obergefell v. Hodges' should be visible in the main item list.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is moved to the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot where the Jurism 'Trash' folder is selected in the left pane, 'Gideon v. Wainwright' should be visible in the main item list.

task_completion.description: Verify 'The Due Process Clause and the Substantive Law of Torts' is moved to the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot where the Jurism 'Trash' folder is selected in the left pane, 'The Due Process Clause and the Substantive Law of Torts' (by Poe) should be visible in the main item list.

task_completion.description: Verify the Trash contains exactly 3 items and was not emptied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the Jurism 'Trash' folder is selected, there should be exactly 3 items listed in the center pane. The trash must not be empty.

task_completion.description: Verify exactly 7 references remain in the active library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When 'My Library' is selected in the left pane of Jurism, there should be exactly 7 items listed in the center pane.

integrity.description: Agent used the Jurism GUI to delete items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking, dragging, or using keyboard shortcuts within the Jurism application window, not typing SQL or terminal commands to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Jurism application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Jurism application interface (which looks similar to Zotero) should be visible with its standard three-pane layout (left pane for collections, center pane for items, right pane for metadata).

integrity.description: Agent did not create fake items to satisfy the count.
integrity.visual_evidence: The items in the library and trash should appear to be the original injected items with proper metadata icons, not newly created empty items just named to match the targets.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jurism database initially contains exactly 10 references. The target state requires exactly 3 specific items in the Trash ('Obergefell v. Hodges', 'Gideon v. Wainwright', and 'The Due Process Clause and the Substantive Law of Torts') and exactly 7 items remaining in 'My Library' (which include 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Marbury v. Madison', 'Miranda v. Arizona', 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan', 'The Path of the Law', 'Constitutional Fact Review', and 'Tinker v. Des Moines').
